mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate and depresses short-term Treasury yields, mechanically increasing the present value of future corporate cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month declines in FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive compared to random walks (t-test p > 0.05) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points over a trailing three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that compresses aggregate income and consumption forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the UNRATE trigger is active during any NBER-dated recession.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month contraction in industrial production and nonfarm payrolls indicates a coordinated collapse in supply-side output and labor income that amplifies earnings depression forecasts beyond either variable alone, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of INDPRO and PAYEMS produces a Rank IC below 0.03 or the wrong sign in a walk-forward regression over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day returns following joint contractions are not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During deep recessions, month-over-month increases in housing starts signal policy rate transmission into the interest-rate-sensitive construction sector and mark an inflection in forward earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: deep recession with zero lower bound monetary policy
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in HOUST do not coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 forward returns in more than 55% of observations during NBER recessions when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production directly foreshadows near-term negative earnings surprises in cyclical sectors, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the production shortfall is priced into equity risk premiums.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for month-over-month INDPRO changes is below 0.03 or has the wrong sign over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate falls below 0.25%, liquidity trap dynamics dominate discount rate effects and policy announcements generate short-term oversold equity bounces, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following months with FEDFUNDS below 0.25% are not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) or are negative in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth captures persistent aggregate demand trends that dominate multi-quarter earnings revision paths, so higher GDP growth relative to the prior quarter predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between quarterly GDPC1 changes and subsequent 60-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling consumer prices combined with a federal funds rate pinned near zero raise ex-post real interest rates and signal deflationary risk that amplifies equity risk premiums beyond either series in isolation, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with deflation risk
failure_condition: The interaction term of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a Rank IC below 0.03 or the wrong sign over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns are not significantly negative when both series jointly signal deflationary pressure.
